Items.Title: Capture accepted baseline for the fixed suite
Items.Hypothesis: A fresh benchmark summary gives the comparison point for all later commits.
Items.Outcome: Release baseline captured from runs/20260410-031655691. Stitch medians: debug slice 16675 ms, synthetic-down 5422 ms, synthetic-up 5352 ms; suite total 27449 ms.

Items.Title: Stop rebuilding the full bidirectional history when zone/start are unchanged
Items.Hypothesis: Once the estimated zone and start frame stabilize, processing only the newest frame should remove the dominant O(n^2) replay cost while preserving the same placement sequence.
Items.Outcome: Kept. Dev-suite stitch medians improved from 16675/5422/5352 ms to 15313/3736/3738 ms (suite total 27449 -> 22787, about 17.0% faster). Full correctness gate stayed exact on both tracked dumps and both synthetic fixtures in runs/20260410-032827482.

Items.Title: Cache adjacent-frame zone and overlap analysis
Items.Hypothesis: Memoizing pairwise zone detection and overlap eligibility across history scans should sharply reduce repeated bitmap extraction and matcher work even when rebuilds still happen.

Items.Title: Remove overlap matcher span-to-array and sub-rectangle allocation churn
Items.Hypothesis: Keeping overlap comparisons in spans/snapshots rather than allocating arrays and sub-buffers per candidate should lower both CPU and GC pressure.

Items.Title: Persist reusable pixel snapshots per captured frame
Items.Hypothesis: Converting each frame bitmap into a reusable snapshot once should cut repeated PixelBuffer.FromBitmap cost across zone detection and overlap search.

Items.Title: Freeze zone estimation after stable consensus and only invalidate on disagreement
Items.Hypothesis: Avoiding full-history zone recomputation after convergence should eliminate wasted rescans on long monotonic captures.

Items.Title: Bound overlap search using recent motion estimates
Items.Hypothesis: Most captures have locally smooth motion, so restricting candidate overlap windows around recent deltas should cut brute-force search cost.

Items.Title: Add a coarse row or column fingerprint pass before pixel refinement
Items.Hypothesis: Cheap 1D fingerprints can reject most impossible overlaps before expensive per-pixel comparisons.

Items.Title: Use rolling hashes or integral summaries for overlap candidates
Items.Hypothesis: Rolling summaries could turn overlap search from repeated full-slice comparisons into cheaper incremental updates.

Items.Title: Replace scalar absolute-difference loops with SIMD
Items.Hypothesis: Vectorized byte-difference kernels should accelerate row, column, and overlap scoring without changing the algorithm.

Items.Title: Prototype a native OpenCV or phase-correlation matcher
Items.Hypothesis: A vision-library backend might deliver a much better accuracy-speed frontier than the current pure C# brute-force matcher.

Items.Title: Evaluate GPU overlap scoring for large scroll bands
Items.Hypothesis: Large overlap scans are embarrassingly parallel and could move well to ComputeSharp, DirectCompute, or CUDA-like kernels.

Items.Title: Swap System.Drawing hot paths for a faster imaging stack
Items.Hypothesis: SkiaSharp, ImageSharp, or a custom native buffer layer could reduce clone, crop, and compose overhead across the whole pipeline.

Items.Title: Parallelize independent pair analysis during history rescans
Items.Hypothesis: Adjacent-pair zone and overlap checks are independent enough to exploit multiple cores when rescans are still necessary.

Items.Title: Move heavy stitching to a separate optimized worker process
Items.Hypothesis: A dedicated worker in another runtime or deployment model could unlock aggressive optimization without entangling the app process.
